text: Convert lume's headline claim from anecdote to data. The claim 'ceremony buys back more time than it costs' (and the second assumption: self-review is honest, indicated by reject rate at the human gate) is named in the review protocol itself as lume's single biggest unproven claim, and docs/questions.md marks both UNMEASURED. No instrumentation exists anywhere in the engine. This is the STANDING value-signal item, unadopted across reviews 2026-06-11-01/-03/-04 and re-surfaced as gap G17. The cheap first cut needs no new hot-path counters: every accept/reject verdict, redo cycle, and iteration transition is already in schema-validated state across all 25+ workstreams. Done-when: a deterministic, read-only, offline report (e.g. a 'lume value' verb or an extension of 'lume status') derives per-workstream and overall verdict statistics - reject rate, accept/redo cycles, iterations-per-workstream, gate-friction - purely from persisted transition history with dates via the existing clock seam; and a logged decision records what the data does and does not say about the two assumptions and what further measurement (if any) is worth building. Scopes to measurement only - it does not change any gate or transition behaviour. This is also the falsifiable-value precondition named on the 0008 autonomy chain.
title: Capture the value signal: reject-rate and gate-friction from lume's own state
